behaviors.description: Conversation history sent to the LLM is limited to the most recent 10 messages when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: history management -> LLM request builder/client. A pure unit test of trimming logic can pass while wiring still sends untrimmed or wrong-ordered messages. No real OS/network is required, and correctness does not depend on model output.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the conversation service with the real history-trimming component and a stub LLM client that captures request payload. Feed >10 prior messages and assert exactly the latest 10 (in correct order) are sent to the client.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher levels rejected: behavior text is about payload handoff, not real HTTP/subprocess or model-quality judgment.

behaviors.description: Generated reply answers the customer’s actual question, not generic/off-topic text.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality correctness of the real LLM. Deterministic stubs cannot expose relevance/off-topic failures. Single model judgment is enough; no required multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real model with fixed conversation contexts and evaluate whether responses address the asked question (using a predefined relevance rubric/labels). Fail when responses are generic or miss the asked intent.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: real HTTP alone is insufficient without real model behavior. Workflow rejected: behavior does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check detects that a reply is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: The phrase "correctly identifies" tone implies semantic judgment quality; mocking that judgment can hide real misclassification. Trigger assertion is orchestration, but detection itself depends on real model-like behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real tone-check path with representative overly formal replies for casual-tier inputs and verify regeneration is triggered. Include borderline samples to catch misclassification drift.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration considered but rejected because behavior text requires correct tone identification, not just wiring. Workflow rejected because this behavior’s core claim is one judgment leading to a trigger, not full multi-step quality verification.

behaviors.description: User message, initial generation, and any regenerations are stored in DB with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: This is persistence/transaction/state propagation across app modules and DB layer. It requires real module wiring plus a real test DB (local/in-process is sufficient). No real external network or model-quality dependence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute chatbot flow with a deterministic LLM stub that forces one regeneration, then query the test DB and assert all exchange records exist with correct linkage/order and expected timestamp fields populated and monotonic.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: behavior text does not require real external DB/network/OS primitives beyond local DB integration.

behaviors.description: If regeneration is triggered, the customer only receives the regenerated response, never the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is state handoff/selection logic after a trigger condition. Minimum failure boundary is orchestration between response pipeline and delivery layer; deterministic stubs can reproduce leakage bugs. It does not require judging model quality when precondition is already 'tone check triggers'.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end service modules with controlled stubs: first draft returned, tone-check forced to trigger, second draft returned. Assert outbound customer payload contains only second draft while audit/persistence can still include first as rejected.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic/Workflow considered and rejected by explicit behavior text: 'When the tone check triggers regeneration' constrains this to post-trigger replacement semantics, not correctness of model decisions.
